name: Tomato and Vegetable Juice Blend
recipeIngredient: Up to 3 cups other vegetables (celery, onions, carrots, peppers)
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush and simmer tomatoes as for making tomato juice.
2. Add up to 3 cups of finely chopped celery, onions, carrots, and peppers for each 22 pounds of tomatoes.
3. Simmer mixture for 20 minutes.
4. Press hot cooked tomatoes and vegetables through a sieve or food mill to remove skins and seeds.
5. Add bottled lemon juice or citric acid to jars (see acidification directions).
6. Add 1 teaspoon salt per quart, if desired.
7. Reheat tomato-vegetable juice blend to boiling.
8. Fill jars immediately, leaving ½-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process as follows:
- Table 1: Hot pack in a boiling-water canner
Pints at altitudes 0–1,000 ft: 35 minutes
Pints at altitudes 1,001–3,000 ft: 40 minutes
Pints at altitudes 3,001–6,000 ft: 45 minutes
Pints above 6,000 ft: 50 minutes
Quarts at all altitudes: Add process times as listed in Table 1
- Table 2: Hot pack in a weighted-gauge pressure canner
Pints: 20 minutes; gauge pressure 5 lb (0–1,000 ft), 10 lb above 1,000 ft
Quarts: 15 minutes; gauge pressure 10 lb (0–1,000 ft), 15 lb above 1,000 ft
- Table 3: Hot pack in a dial-gauge pressure canner
Pints: 20 minutes; gauge pressure 6–9 lb depending on altitude
Quarts: 15 minutes; gauge pressure 11–14 lb depending on altitude
